text: This 100 days of code is running like Netflix People are coming to watch it daily like a TV series It's fun In today's video we are going to talk about string methods We read about string We saw what is a string We saw that it is a very important data type We also saw that what are the other ways to make a string Today I will tell you how operations can be done with a string For example, you want to convert a string in upper case Suppose you want to convert the whole string in lower case You want to find the length of a string How to find it? I will tell you all these things in this video Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started As you can see I have come to my Apple If you have not accessed the playlist yet Then do access it or click and bookmark Or click and save Today we are going to see some string methods I have written here a is equal to Harry This is a 5 length string I know we saw in the last video That we can print len of a in this way And python will give us 5 There was no big deal in it We also got 5 But what will I do if I want to convert this string in upper case The first thing I want to explain to you is the concept of immutability I want to explain this to you The strings are immutable You can't change them But you can change them It's not that you can't change them You can't change them in place Let me explain Suppose I have a is equal to Harry I can't change this variable as it is What is made is made I can make a new copy of it When I apply any string method Suppose I write here a.upper I get a new string I get a new string In which all the characters are in upper case As you can see it has come in Harry capital It's not that the old string has changed This question comes in the interview That's why I am telling you That the strings are immutable I write here to remind you That strings are immutable Strings are immutable And I will make a comment With the help of control forward slash So that it doesn't execute Because if it executes It will give an error And I don't want it to give an error Okay As I told you about upper method Similarly, there is a lower method Which converts our string to lower case And it's very obvious That the lower method converts a string to lower case I have given an example here In case you want to try it Will this example convert string 1 to lower case Do you know what is the answer to this question It won't do Why it won't do Because it will make a new string It won't change the existing string Strings are immutable in python You can't change them Okay But you can make a new string By using string methods So what do string methods do They operate on your existing string And return a new string So we saw this thing a.upper a.lower a.lower will convert entire Harry to lower case It was H Now it's h Okay Now there is a method called rstrip Which removes trailing characters Suppose I had written something like this in my name Okay, Harry So if I print a.upper, a.lower Or a for that matter Then I will get my string directly But I want If I get this exclamation mark as a strip Then I will do a.rstrip Okay, what does it do It strips Who does it strip The trailing characters So I can strip the exclamation mark Like this You can see here It's stripped Does it strip the leading? Does it strip the leading exclamation mark? The answer is no The exclamation will be there in the starting As you can see It strips only the trailing Okay So we have learned this Okay I will leave it like this To Harry So that you can understand this rstrip Now let's talk about replace What does it do It replaces all occurrence of a string with another string Suppose I want to write john instead of harry Okay So I will write a.replace Replace harry with what And I will obviously write in double quote Replace all the occurrences of harry With john So if I print this Then this string will be printed And john will be written here As you can see Okay What if there were 2-3 more Harrys here Would they also change? Yes, all occurrences are written So all Harrys will change to john As you can see a.replace harry It became john This was harry harry It became john Okay So this is the thing Now what does split do? Split is a very good method Because what it does That your This string Converts it into a list Now we have not read so much about the list So I will not talk much about this method But I will show you If I Split Split with a space And my string should also have space Then it will split So this will be the first element of the list This will be the second element of the list If there is space somewhere else Then this will be the third element of the list That is, this This And this will become a list I will show you how this thing works If I run this Then a list will be made In that will come harry Then this Then this harry As you can see here What is a list?
